flashcards.front: What is the root interface of the Java Collections Framework?
flashcards.back: The Collection interface is the root interface of the Java Collections Framework hierarchy.

flashcards.front: Which Collection interface allows positional access to elements?
flashcards.back: The List interface allows positional access to elements using methods like get(index).

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between List and Set interfaces?
flashcards.back: List allows duplicate elements and maintains insertion order, while Set prohibits duplicates and doesn't guarantee order.

flashcards.front: What does FIFO stand for in the context of Queue interface?
flashcards.back: FIFO stands for First-In-First-Out, meaning the first element added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.

flashcards.front: Which Collection interface would you use to model a mathematical set?
flashcards.back: The Set interface should be used to model a mathematical set as it prohibits duplicate elements.
